items.title: Hobart 'Stadium 2.0' developer's company in liquidation, as he works on new waterfront project

children.content: Tasmanian developer Dean Coleman's company, which was involved in the rejected 'Stadium 2.0' plan for Hobart, is in liquidation. He's now involved in a separate development proposal at Regatta Point.

gallery.altText: An artist's impression of a round silver stadium jutting into the water, surrounded by green space, with Hobart city behind.

thumbnail.altText: An artist's impression of a round silver stadium jutting into the water, surrounded by green space, with Hobart city behind.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Aboriginal activist tells court of obligation to prevent 'slaughtered' forests

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

image.altText: People gathered outside court with an Aboriginal flag

items.title: Sale of Tasmania's biggest farm to UK pine plantation investor approved

children.content: The sale of Tasmania's biggest farm to the UK's largest forestry investor has been given the green light — with the news greeted by outrage from the state's farmers' group.

image.altText: dairy cows graze on paddocks

items.title: Firefighter charged over alleged possession of bestiality material

children.content: A 37-year-old firefighter from southern Tasmania has been charged with possessing bestiality material.

image.altText: The Tasmanian Fire Service logo on the side of a truck.

items.title: Staff at award-winning agency find lock-out notice on front door

children.content: Staff of an award-winning branding agency say they arrived at work one day to locked doors and a notice saying their employer was $25,000 behind on rent, and it went downhill from there.

image.altText: A letter posted to a glass office door saying $25,168 is owed in rent.

items.title: Cleary 'silences the critics' as NSW make Origin history with decider win

children.content: NSW has produced a historic performance to win the State of Origin series with a win over Queensland at Lang Park in Brisbane.

articles.title: The other edge cuts back on the Maroons in shock Origin loss

children.content: The Maroons under Billy Slater have gone all in on speed, attack and flair. On the biggest stage in the Origin decider, when things went against them, it all turned a bit too frantic.

image.altText: Queensland Maroons players watch Nathan Cleary line up another goal in State of Origin.

articles.title: New South Wales writes a new fate in blood after epic decider

children.content: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

image.altText: A rugby league player in blue holds up his hands in celebration

articles.title: Quick hits: Arrow's standing 'O' and Queensland 'ripped off'

children.content: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

image.altText: Reece Walsh, Jai Arrow and Nathan Cleary in State of Origin III 2026.

items.title: As snowfall declines, a key ingredient is keeping the ski season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

children.content: The ABC and SBS will face questioning over complaints of bias and antisemitism when executives appear at the royal commission on Thursday.

items.title: Here's how the Telstra outage impacted these Australians

children.content: Millions have potentially been impacted by the Telstra outage. Here's how it affected Australians doing basic everyday things, from making calls to measuring blood sugar levels.

items.title: Melbourne injecting room cut ambulance callouts by 70pc, report reveals

children.content: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

image.altText: A close up of a syringe and needle

items.title: World Cup quarterfinals are upon us. Here's what you need to know

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

items.title: Qld business fined $1,200 after sparking million-dollar fire ant scare
